Run content-level lint checks (titles, alt text, broken local links, future dates, duplicate slugs, malformed frontmatter)

Usage:
  nectar lint [--config <path>] [--json] [--strict] [--max-title-length <n>]

Options:
  -c, --config <path> Path to a config file; disables discovery when set
  -j, --json          Emit findings as JSON ({ count, findings: [{ rule, severity, file, message }] }) for CI consumption
  --strict            Exit with non-zero status if any warning-level findings were emitted (errors always exit non-zero)
  --max-title-length <n>  Override the max title length before a warning is emitted (default: 70 characters; Google SERP cut-off rule of thumb)
  -h, --help          Show help for this command

Environment variables:
  Every flag has an env var fallback (CLI flag > env var > config > default).
  Naming: NECTAR_<COMMAND>_<FLAG> (uppercased, dashes become underscores).
  Example: --config → NECTAR_LINT_CONFIG

Examples:
  nectar lint                                  # warn-level summary table
  nectar lint --strict                         # exit non-zero on any warning
  nectar lint --json | jq                      # CI-friendly findings stream
  nectar lint --max-title-length 60
